programme.title: Mapped Out
programme.desc: Nationwide blackouts, fuel shortages and food running out: Cuba is in crisis. The US has cut off oil to the Caribbean nation, including from Venezuela and Mexico, previously Cuba's two main oil suppliers.

programme.title: The 77 Percent
programme.desc: In this week's show, Edith Kimani meets with Afro-Americans who have relocated to Ghana and locals to explore the challenges and opportunities sparked by the 'Year of Return'.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: All organisms harbor an invisible, omnipresent enemy: plastic. Researchers worldwide have shown that plastic particles can also be detected in humans - in organs and bodily fluids.

programme.title: Made in Germany
programme.desc: Munich is emerging as a hub for drone innovation: Bundeswehr University start-ups are building drones that conquer the skies and can also see underground.

programme.title: Mapped Out
programme.desc: Nationwide blackouts, fuel shortages and food running out: Cuba is in crisis. The US has cut off oil to the Caribbean nation, including from Venezuela and Mexico, previously Cuba's two main oil suppliers.

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: More and more women are speaking out about violence in relationships, and are demanding stricter laws, including for deep fakes. +++ Storms are wreaking ever more damage on Italy's coasts. There is often insufficient coastal protection.

programme.title: Arts Unveiled
programme.desc: Is Joseph Haydn's music boring? Not at all, say Paavo Järvi and the musicians of the Deutsche Kammerphilharmonie Bremen. On their musical journey through Haydn's symphonic works, they discover new and surprising things.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: More than 140 years after its invention, the car remains a source of fascination for many people. In Germany, for example, there are more vehicles on the road than ever before. For some, the car is a cult object. But it also deeply polarizing.

programme.title: Made in Germany
programme.desc: Munich is emerging as a hub for drone innovation: Bundeswehr University start-ups are building drones that conquer the skies and can also see underground.

programme.title: Mapped Out
programme.desc: Nationwide blackouts, fuel shortages and food running out: Cuba is in crisis. The US has cut off oil to the Caribbean nation, including from Venezuela and Mexico, previously Cuba's two main oil suppliers.

programme.title: Eco India
programme.desc: In the Western Ghats, locals are learning that tending forests is profitable. Also: green jobs, environmental storytelling, and a river clean-up in Germany.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: More than 140 years after its invention, the car remains a source of fascination for many people. In Germany, for example, there are more vehicles on the road than ever before. For some, the car is a cult object. But it also deeply polarizing.

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: More and more women are speaking out about violence in relationships, and are demanding stricter laws, including for deep fakes. +++ Storms are wreaking ever more damage on Italy's coasts. There is often insufficient coastal protection.

programme.title: Mapped Out
programme.desc: Nationwide blackouts, fuel shortages and food running out: Cuba is in crisis. The US has cut off oil to the Caribbean nation, including from Venezuela and Mexico, previously Cuba's two main oil suppliers.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: 2024 was a record year for Norwegian tourism, with almost 38.6 million overnight stays. The country's cuisine, culture, and unique destinations entice visitors. But tourism brings its own problems.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: All organisms harbor an invisible, omnipresent enemy: plastic. Researchers worldwide have shown that plastic particles can also be detected in humans - in organs and bodily fluids.

programme.title: Made in Germany
programme.desc: Munich is emerging as a hub for drone innovation: Bundeswehr University start-ups are building drones that conquer the skies and can also see underground.

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: More and more women are speaking out about violence in relationships, and are demanding stricter laws, including for deep fakes. +++ Storms are wreaking ever more damage on Italy's coasts. There is often insufficient coastal protection.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Constant stimuli, digital distractions - how can we stay focused these days? Does talent, training, or technology help? Neuroscientists and mental coaches show how concentration works in the brain.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: More than 140 years after its invention, the car remains a source of fascination for many people. In Germany, for example, there are more vehicles on the road than ever before. For some, the car is a cult object. But it also deeply polarizing.

programme.title: Sports Life
programme.desc: In Assam, decades of conflict kept Bodo and Santhal communities apart. Now the traditional martial art Khomlainai is bringing children together, who have never spoken or played with each other before. Mejing Narzary, a former Bodo underground fighter, and Khermu Karmakar, a former general of the Santhal insurgent force, have achieved this miracle.

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: More and more women are speaking out about violence in relationships, and are demanding stricter laws, including for deep fakes. +++ Storms are wreaking ever more damage on Italy's coasts. There is often insufficient coastal protection.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: All organisms harbor an invisible, omnipresent enemy: plastic. Researchers worldwide have shown that plastic particles can also be detected in humans - in organs and bodily fluids.

programme.title: Business Beyond
programme.desc: After decades of stalled talks, Europe has reached a trade turning point: It's been on a deal signing spree, inking mammoth pacts with major powers like India, Indonesia, Australia, Brazil and Argentina. This is a story about how the world recalibrates when the US goes rogue, and the race to rely less on China.

programme.title: REV
programme.desc: In Venezuela, years of halted imports, sky-high tariffs, and economic collapse have left drivers stuck between aging Toyotas and pricey Chinese SUVs in a distorted market. Plus: REV puts the new Mazda 6e to the test.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Constant stimuli, digital distractions - how can we stay focused these days? Does talent, training, or technology help? Neuroscientists and mental coaches show how concentration works in the brain.

programme.title: In Good Shape
programme.desc: What's the best skincare routine for your skin type? Plus: AI-assisted skin screenings, removing unwanted tattoos, and tackling herpes outbreaks.

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: More and more women are speaking out about violence in relationships, and are demanding stricter laws, including for deep fakes. +++ Storms are wreaking ever more damage on Italy's coasts. There is often insufficient coastal protection.

programme.title: Business Beyond
programme.desc: After decades of stalled talks, Europe has reached a trade turning point: It's been on a deal signing spree, inking mammoth pacts with major powers like India, Indonesia, Australia, Brazil and Argentina. This is a story about how the world recalibrates when the US goes rogue, and the race to rely less on China.

programme.title: Global Us
programme.desc: An NGO has been planting araucaria trees for 40 years to protect Brazil's Atlantic rainforest. In India, the AI boom is leading to increasing conflicts over water supply.

programme.title: Business Beyond
programme.desc: After decades of stalled talks, Europe has reached a trade turning point: It's been on a deal signing spree, inking mammoth pacts with major powers like India, Indonesia, Australia, Brazil and Argentina. This is a story about how the world recalibrates when the US goes rogue, and the race to rely less on China.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Constant stimuli, digital distractions - how can we stay focused these days? Does talent, training, or technology help? Neuroscientists and mental coaches show how concentration works in the brain.

programme.title: Business Beyond
programme.desc: After decades of stalled talks, Europe has reached a trade turning point: It's been on a deal signing spree, inking mammoth pacts with major powers like India, Indonesia, Australia, Brazil and Argentina. This is a story about how the world recalibrates when the US goes rogue, and the race to rely less on China.

programme.title: Best of DW Podcasts
programme.desc: Select DW podcasts enrich the program with current analyses of world politics and its influence on our daily lives. There will also be space in the program for lively discussions about social standards and taboos.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: India's widows suffer from exclusion and stigmatization. For decades, they have been fighting for more rights, with the help of NGOs. For the first time, widows are allowed to celebrate the festival of colors at the Gopinath Temple - a breakthrough.

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: More and more women are speaking out about violence in relationships, and are demanding stricter laws, including for deep fakes. +++ Storms are wreaking ever more damage on Italy's coasts. There is often insufficient coastal protection.

programme.title: Business Beyond
programme.desc: After decades of stalled talks, Europe has reached a trade turning point: It's been on a deal signing spree, inking mammoth pacts with major powers like India, Indonesia, Australia, Brazil and Argentina. This is a story about how the world recalibrates when the US goes rogue, and the race to rely less on China.

programme.desc: Munich is emerging as a hub for drone innovation: Bundeswehr University start-ups are building drones that conquer the skies and can also see underground.

programme.desc: India's widows suffer from exclusion and stigmatization. For decades, they have been fighting for more rights, with the help of NGOs. For the first time, widows are allowed to celebrate the festival of colors at the Gopinath Temple - a breakthrough.

This is a story about how the world recalibrates when the US goes rogue, and the race to rely less on China.

programme.desc: Constant stimuli, digital distractions - how can we stay focused these days? Does talent, training, or technology help? Neuroscientists and mental coaches show how concentration works in the brain.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: More than 140 years after its invention, the car remains a source of fascination for many people. In Germany, for example, there are more vehicles on the road than ever before. For some, the car is a cult object. But it also deeply polarizing.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: India's widows suffer from exclusion and stigmatization. For decades, they have been fighting for more rights, with the help of NGOs. For the first time, widows are allowed to celebrate the festival of colors at the Gopinath Temple - a breakthrough.

programme.title: Best of DW Podcasts
programme.desc: Select DW podcasts enrich the program with current analyses of world politics and its influence on our daily lives. There will also be space in the program for lively discussions about social standards and taboos.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Constant stimuli, digital distractions - how can we stay focused these days? Does talent, training, or technology help? Neuroscientists and mental coaches show how concentration works in the brain.

programme.title: Travel
programme.desc: Solo travel is on the rise — but what does it really take to explore the world on your own, especially as a woman? In this episode, global explorer Eva zu Beck and DW Travel hosts share their personal insights and experiences from years of solo travel. Be sure to watch until the end, when Eva zu Beck, Hanna Hummel, Sarah Hucal, Aisha Sharipzhan and Gönna Ketels reveal their most helpful tips.

programme.title: In Good Shape
programme.desc: Diet breads for weight loss, magic exercises for strong muscles, secret tricks for skin and hair: Many tips on the web are ineffective, some are dangerous, but others are promising. In Good Shape has put some trends under a microscope.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: More than 140 years after its invention, the car remains a source of fascination for many people. In Germany, for example, there are more vehicles on the road than ever before. For some, the car is a cult object. But it also deeply polarizing.

programme.title: Eco India
programme.desc: Retrofitting conventional vehicles could help Delhi go electric more equitably. Also: women farmers battle age-old structures — and making power from bird poop.

programme.title: Travel
programme.desc: Solo travel is on the rise — but what does it really take to explore the world on your own, especially as a woman? In this episode, global explorer Eva zu Beck and DW Travel hosts share their personal insights and experiences from years of solo travel. Be sure to watch until the end, when Eva zu Beck, Hanna Hummel, Sarah Hucal, Aisha Sharipzhan and Gönna Ketels reveal their most helpful tips.

programme.title: Afrimaxx
programme.desc: Across the continent, African creatives are claiming the space reshaping the way we live, remember, and express ourselves. And that's why today we are visiting the Investec Cape Town Art Fair in South Africa.

programme.title: Best of DW Podcasts
programme.desc: Select DW podcasts enrich the program with current analyses of world politics and its influence on our daily lives. There will also be space in the program for lively discussions about social standards and taboos.
